title: For any fans in Mexico Rebuild 10 as part of Anifest
display_title: For any fans in Mexico Rebuild 10 as part of Anifest
wikitext:
[]: For any fans in Mexico. Rebuild 1.0 as part of Anifest             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Aug 28, 2010 3:21 am Tickets are pre-sale only, apparently. It's showing/will show on: Cinemark Monterey Plaza Real The Girl Who Lept Through Time: 2:00pm Final Fantasy VII: Advent Children 4:00pm EVANGELION 1.0: 6:30pm And then in Cinemark Guadalajara, from September 3rd to September 5th. Exact same showtimes. Keep an eye out for this page in case of any changes: http://www.cinemark.com.mx/pelicula.php?pelicula=2578 I'll seriously try to go. TGWHTT and 1.0 on the same day at the cinema...cracked up to be one of the best days ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinies » Sun Aug 29, 2010 2:36 pm Damn it, no Mexico City.... Anyway, are there any plans to bring 1.11 here on blu-ray? I haven't heard anything and it seems Spain already got a dubbed release.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sun Aug 29, 2010 3:49 pm Shinies wrote: Damn it, no Mexico City.... Anyway, are there any plans to bring 1.11 here on blu-ray? I haven't heard anything and it seems Spain already got a dubbed release.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Sun Aug 29, 2010 11:37 pm Dubbed or subbed? I didn't like the dubbed version on the ADV DVDs and if it's the same quality with the rebuild I might skip it. As far as I know there is no good spanish dub version of Eva and I've never liked castellian dubbing so that's a no ether way. Well, untill they show up in Tijuana I won't be able to see it anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Mon Aug 30, 2010 2:48 pm berto wrote: Dubbed or subbed? I didn't like the dubbed version on the ADV DVDs and if it's the same quality with the rebuild I might skip it. As far as I know there is no good spanish dub version of Eva and I've never liked castellian dubbing so that's a no ether way. Well, untill they show up in Tijuana I won't be able to see it anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Aug 30, 2010 6:04 pm berto wrote: Dubbed or subbed? I didn't like the dubbed version on the ADV DVDs and if it's the same quality with the rebuild I might skip it. As far as I know there is no good spanish dub version of Eva and I've never liked castellian dubbing so that's a no ether way. Well, untill they show up in Tijuana I won't be able to see it anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Aug 30, 2010 10:23 pm NOOOO! Why it's always there! Never my city! Never good movies! Never Cirque du Soleil! Never decent theater plays! Only once in a while some classic music performance! Never a Broadway musical! @shinji_ryoji_89 : it's not "screwy"... Is beyond fucked up! It was killed, dismembered and fucked! There has not been a decently dubbed anime since Card Captor Sakura! Almost 10 years ago!

[]:             Quote Post by Shinies » Tue Aug 31, 2010 2:58 am Mmm I actually liked the dubbed version. Must be the only one. Seems to me that internet spoiled many with the dub thing, back when we were kids we didn't complained about that, and as far as I know mexican dubs are most of the time the best spanish dubs. A good example of that is the voice of Goku, with the Japanese one sounding like a girl and the mexican one more like a man. Whatever I just one at least a subbed blu-ray release here, is that much to ask?

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Tue Aug 31, 2010 2:38 pm arael wrote: NOOOO! Why it's always there! Never my city! Never good movies! Never Cirque du Soleil! Never decent theater plays! Only once in a while some classic music performance! Never a Broadway musical! @shinji_ryoji_89 : it's not "screwy"... Is beyond fucked up! It was killed, dismembered and fucked! There has not been a decently dubbed anime since Card Captor Sakura! Almost 10 years ago!

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Tue Aug 31, 2010 7:28 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: there was sort of anime discrediting campaign in the mainstream media around that time and that really screwed things up I think it is making a comeback.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Sep 01, 2010 10:50 am Yes, you are completely right. It's a real shame. And it's a problem not only with anime, but with even most films in general now. Inception was nearly a victim of the whole distribution fiasco, luckily people spoke out. Anyway, I really hope I can go to this event. I feel pretty sad that the only anime movies I've seen in the theater have were "Pokémon: The First Movie" and "Dragon Ball Z: The Tree of Might" : (
